authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR 121.533 - Responsibility for operational control: Domestic operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This FAA regulation allocates operational-control responsibility in domestic air-carrier operations, making it authoritative for refusing support-lane actions that would imply dispatch or operational-control authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: intake classification, authority boundary review, dispatch-owner routing, and refusal control
used_for: refusal when support work implies flight release authority

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR 121.593 - Dispatching authority: Domestic operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This regulation governs dispatch authorization for domestic operations, making it authoritative for the lane's rule that support summaries cannot substitute for a dispatch release.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: dispatch dependency review, support resolution, and adjacent-owner escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR 121.687 - Dispatch release: Flag and domestic operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This rule defines dispatch-release content for flag and domestic operations, making it authoritative for flight number, aircraft, route, alternate, fuel, ETOPS, weather, and forecast evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: prerequisite validation, evidence packaging, disruption support, and documentation-gap handling

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR 121.695 - Load manifest: All certificate holders
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This air-carrier rule establishes load-manifest record expectations, making it authoritative for evidence handling around load, passenger, cargo, and movement documentation without granting load-control approval authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: load and movement evidence review, handoff packaging, and closure evidence control

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 65 Subpart C - Aircraft Dispatchers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA aircraft dispatcher certification rules define licensed dispatcher qualifications and boundaries, grounding the refusal to act as a dispatcher or provide licensed dispatch judgment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authority classification, licensed-role escalation, and refusal handling

authoritative_sources.title: FAA Order 7930.2U - Notice to Airmen (NOTAM)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA NOTAM order guidance defines how unanticipated or temporary NAS changes are formatted and disseminated, making it authoritative for route-status, airport-condition, and service-disruption support packaging.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: route and schedule disruption intake, NOTAM dependency review, and irregular-operations escalation

authoritative_sources.title: AC 120-92D - Safety Management Systems for Aviation Service Providers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA SMS guidance explains aviation hazard identification, risk mitigation, assurance, and safety decision support, making it authoritative for escalating safety-related requests rather than resolving them as service tickets.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safety signal triage, hazard escalation, irregular-operations support, and refusal control

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR 122.48a - Electronic information for air cargo required in advance of arrival
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This CBP regulation defines advance electronic air cargo information requirements and supports escalation when support work depends on customs documentation or clearance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: customs documentation prerequisite validation, cargo movement support, and customs-owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 1544 - Aircraft Operator Security
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TSA aircraft operator security rules define security-program boundaries, supporting escalation rather than support-lane decisions when disruption or movement depends on security handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: security-sensitive exception handling, adjacent-owner routing, and refusal control

authoritative_sources.title: IATA Baggage Tracking - Resolution 753
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATA Resolution 753 defines baggage tracking points and audit-trail expectations used by airlines and handlers to establish acquisition, load, transfer, and arrival custody visibility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: baggage custody review, connection exception handling, handoff evidence capture, and passenger-impact escalation

authoritative_sources.title: IATA Ground Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATA ground operations references are recognized airline and ground-handler operating standards for safe and efficient ground handling, turnaround dependencies, GSE, baggage, and service sequencing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: turnaround support, station dependency packaging, equipment blocker handling, and service-level communication

workflow_stages.name: Support intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Normalize the requested outcome, affected flight leg, route, station, schedule, passenger-impact, cargo or baggage movement, and requested action; classify as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked before support continues.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and system-of-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate flight operations logs, dispatch record references, flight plan or release references, schedule and route records, weather and NOTAM context, load manifests, custody records, maintenance status, customs documentation, security constraints, service policies, and tenant owner maps.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Evidence-backed support resolution
workflow_stages.description: Resolve supported requests by summarizing known facts, action taken, stakeholder message, unresolved dependencies, and next actions without making dispatch, customs, maintenance, security, safety, legal, or financial decisions.
source_classes: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Movement, turnaround, and chain-of-custody coordination
workflow_stages.description: Package movement and turnaround status across flight, tail, route, gate, stand, crew-connectivity, load, baggage, cargo, ULD, fuel, GSE, maintenance, customs, and handoff dependencies while preserving source-system traceability.
source_classes: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Irregular-operations and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when schedule disruption, route changes, weather, NOTAMs, ATC flow, customs gaps, maintenance status, safety signals, security constraints, or passenger-impact commitments threaten compliant movement or support closure.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and handoff closure
workflow_stages.description: Confirm support resolution only when the request summary, action taken, communication or handoff, evidence references, unresolved items, and next accountable owner are explicit; otherwise produce a blocked or escalation package.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references

artifacts: flight operations log, AOC notes, station notes, service ticket, shift briefing, flight-following record, and irregular-operations case
flight number, leg, tail, aircraft type, origin, destination, alternates, route, ETD, ETA, delay code, service level, and passenger-impact records
dispatch release reference, operational flight plan, weather package, METAR/TAF summaries, NOTAM references, ATC flow or NAS status notes, and route constraints
load manifest, weight and balance reference, fuel record, MEL or CDL reference, maintenance status, return-to-service reference, and aircraft status record
booking, shipment, airway bill, cargo manifest, ULD record, baggage scans, baggage reconciliation, handoff record, chain-of-custody log, and destination acknowledgement
gate, stand, ramp, fueling, deicing, catering, cleaning, cabin, GSE, fleet, telematics, security, customs documentation, customer operations, and service logs
systems_of_record: airline operations control or flight operations log
flight following, flight status, AODB, FIDS, gate, and station operations systems
weather, NOTAM, NAS status, and route advisory systems
booking, cargo, airway bill, ULD, baggage reconciliation, BRS, BHS, TMS, and WMS systems
load control, weight and balance, fuel, maintenance, fleet, telematics, GSE, and deicing systems
customs documentation systems, security systems, customer operations CRM, service desk, and escalation queue

allowed: classify a support request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked
request or validate minimum facts and source records needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, stakeholder communication summaries, documentation-gap notes, movement or turnaround summaries, and escalation packets
route work to the correct adjacent specialist or human reviewer when ownership shifts
apply documented tenant service rules when the record set is complete and no authority boundary is crossed
escalate: movement depends on dispatch release, operational control, ATC flow decision, customs release, security handling, maintenance return-to-service, safety risk acceptance, or legal interpretation
required records are missing, stale, contradictory, or outside the lane's retrieval scope
weather, NOTAM, route, schedule, customs, maintenance, or safety conditions threaten compliant movement
public sources and tenant procedures materially conflict
repeat failure, unsupported workaround, customer-impact exception, or policy override request needs an accountable owner
refuse: request asks the lane to issue or amend a dispatch release, approve a flight plan, clear a flight, or exercise operational control
request asks the lane to clear customs, release held cargo, approve security handling, or make legal determinations
request asks the lane to approve maintenance return-to-service, safety signoff, deicing release, MEL or CDL applicability, fuel requirement, or airworthiness decision
request asks for policy, pricing, eligibility, compensation, or financial approval override
request asks to close support work without evidence of action taken, communication or handoff, unresolved items, and next owner

domain_failure_modes: flight movement disruption from stale route, weather, NOTAM, ATC flow, or schedule assumptions
chain-of-custody failure from missing ULD, baggage, cargo, booking, airway bill, or destination handoff evidence
customs or documentation noncompliance from missing air cargo data, customs status, or release authority
service failure from bad routing, scheduling, passenger-impact, or station handoff assumptions
unsafe overreach from implying dispatch, maintenance, deicing, security, operational-control, or safety authority
generic support behavior that ignores aviation artifacts, systems of record, and regulated movement boundaries

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Route or schedule disruption with compliance impact
Request that implies customs, dispatch, or safety authority
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant operations specifications and dispatch procedures
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Carrier-specific operations specifications, dispatch procedures, operational-control matrices, and station SOPs materially narrow the public baseline and must be retrieved at runtime.
International routes may require jurisdiction-specific customs, immigration, security, and airport rules; the lane escalates rather than guessing when those sources are not supplied.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because all tenant-specific ambiguity is converted into explicit runtime prerequisite, refusal, or escalation behavior.
Research constrains the pack toward support resolution, evidence packaging, communication, and handoff rather than dispatch release, customs clearance, maintenance signoff, or safety authority.
